column1: # Negative-control registry — consumed by specs/negative_controls.sh.
# One row per control, TAB-separated:
#   <id>  <patch>  <module.tla>  <config.cfg>  <expected red>
# Column 3 is the module TLC CHECKS, which is not always the file the patch
# MUTATES (the patch records its own target): NC-3's patch breaks
# specs/common/Footprint.tla but is witnessed by CommitProtocol against
# CommitAccurate.cfg. The staging must therefore propagate a patched
# specs/common/Footprint.tla into specs/commit/ and specs/scheduler/ (in the
# repo those are symlinks; a scratch copy that materializes them BEFORE
# patching leaves the commit-local copy pristine and NC-3 stays green).
# Expected-red values: an invariant name (check_expected.sh greps
# "Invariant <Name> is violated"), DEADLOCK ("Deadlock reached"), or
# ASSUMPTION:<Name> — a named ASSUME that fails at TLC startup with no state
# graph. TLC reports an assumption failure by LOCATION, not by name; the
# current phrasing for NC-7 is:
#   Error: Assumption line 155, col 5 to line 156, col 66 of module FootprintLemmas is false.
# (the line span is LemmaCoverageIsSound's body in FootprintLemmas.tla —
# derive it from the file rather than hardcoding if that file is edited).
# Patches are cut against the current specs tree with >=5 context lines and
# apply with either `git apply` (repo root) or `patch -p1`. Regenerate any
# patch whose mutation site is edited: re-break a fresh copy, `diff -U8`.
# Not here, by design: NC-8 (structural — the dirty-check deletion itself,
# pinned as the CoverageSubsumesDirty invariant on every push). NC-2's
# ORIGINAL oracle (the DirtyRestart action-coverage count) died with the
# dirty check, but its break — reverting the H3 fix — lives on below with a
# red-verdict oracle instead.
NC-1
NC-2
NC-3
NC-4
NC-5
NC-6
NC-7
NC-A
NC-B
NC-C
